# dokploy_email_notification (Resource)

An Email (SMTP) notification configuration.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "dokploy_email_notification" "alerts" {
  name         = "ops-team"
  smtp_server  = "smtp.example.com"
  smtp_port    = 587
  username     = var.smtp_user
  password     = var.smtp_password
  from_address = "alerts@example.com"
  to_addresses = ["ops@example.com"]
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `from_address` (String) From email address.
- `name` (String) Display name.
- `password` (String, Sensitive) SMTP password.
- `smtp_port` (Number) SMTP server port.
- `smtp_server` (String) SMTP server hostname.
- `to_addresses` (List of String) Recipient email addresses.
- `username` (String) SMTP username.

### Optional

- `app_build_error` (Boolean) Notify on build error.
- `app_deploy` (Boolean) Notify on application deploy.
- `database_backup` (Boolean) Notify on database backup events.
- `docker_cleanup` (Boolean) Notify on Docker cleanup.
- `dokploy_backup` (Boolean) Notify on Dokploy self-backup events.
- `dokploy_restart` (Boolean) Notify on Dokploy restart.
- `server_threshold` (Boolean) Notify on server resource threshold breaches.
- `volume_backup` (Boolean) Notify on volume backup events.

### Read-Only

- `email_id` (String) Email-specific sub-ID used for updates.
- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import dokploy_email_notification.alerts <notificationId>
```
